checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked the current public contract at [src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:1) and the underlying return type in [src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:107). The plan's Step 4 says the adapter will "likely" preserve some legacy fields and the metadata questions ask whether only currently used callers matter, but the milestone brief requires the public return shape to stay unchanged; the existing surface includes `entityId`, `isDirty`, `hasShotSettings`, `hasPersistedData`, `updateTextField`, `updateTextFields`, `saveImmediate(dataToSave?)`, `reset(newDefaults?)`, and `initializeFrom`, so leaving that list unresolved is a real requirement gap.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked [src/shared/hooks/settings/useToolSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/hooks/settings/useToolSettings.ts:1) and the shared runtime exports in [src/shared/settings/index.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/index.ts:1). `useToolSettings` is a React hook, so it cannot be called from the plain async `load` / `save` callbacks that `createEntityStore` expects at module scope; the plan says the Join Clips port should use the existing `useToolSettings` / Supabase mechanism underneath, but unless it explicitly routes through non-hook helpers like `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` and `updateToolSettingsSupabase` plus a hook-side query adapter, that architecture is not executable as written.

Checked the draft M1 files at [src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:1) and [src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts:1). The current draft auto-defers `textFieldKeys` even when `updateField` is used, and the test suite currently expects `updateField('text', ...)` not to save; that conflicts with the milestone brief, but the plan explicitly calls out this mismatch and proposes correcting it, so that particular draft inconsistency is already identified rather than missed.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Searched the actual Join Clips consumers in [src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:84), [src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsGenerate.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsGenerate.ts:43), and [src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts:140). The brief's "smallest consumer, 5 fields" framing is stale for this checkout: current callers depend on far more than five fields, including prompts, clip arrays, LoRA/motion config, resolution/FPS toggles, model/seed settings, and preset IDs, so the migration scope is still one hook but it must preserve the full `JoinClipsSettings` shape rather than validate only a tiny subset.

Checked the broader load path behavior in [src/shared/settings/hooks/autoSaveSettingsLoaders.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/settings/hooks/autoSaveSettingsLoaders.ts:41). `useAutoSaveSettings` is not just a one-time load/save wrapper; in React Query mode it reacts to cache refreshes and explicitly avoids overwriting pending local edits, so a factory that only performs lazy `load(entityId)` on first mount would narrow behavior unless the hook adapter also owns query-cache subscription and invalidation handling.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: Grepped `ReturnType<typeof useJoinClipsSettings>` and direct uses of the hook. Beyond [src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts:1), downstream code in [src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsGenerate.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsGenerate.ts:25), [src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts:32), [src/tools/join-clips/pages/hooks/useJoinClipsPageHelpers.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/hooks/useJoinClipsPageHelpers.ts:15), and travel-between-images adapters/types all compile against that return type. The plan only names the hook test update, so it underestimates the supporting verification surface that will fail if the adapter omits fields or changes method signatures.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: Checked the actual setter call sites in [src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:96) and the shared form prop builders under travel-between-images. Prompt-like fields such as `prompt`, `negativePrompt`, and `enhancePrompt` are all currently updated through `joinSettings.updateField(...)`, and there is no existing blur handler or `flushTextFields` plumbing for Join Clips; if M1 marks those keys as `textFieldKeys` without also rewiring callers, prompt edits will stop participating in the current debounced save path.

Checked the hook entry points in [src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:248) and the current implementation in [src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:9). Callers pass `selectedProjectId` or nullable project IDs, not shot IDs, and the current hook disables persistence when that value is nullish; the plan's Step 4 already switches to project-scoped semantics and explicitly preserves null/undefined disabled behavior, so that caller shape appears covered.

flags.concern: Settings adapter surface: the plan leaves the required unchanged `useJoinClipsSettings` return shape unresolved even though the current hook publicly re-exports the full `useAutoSaveSettings` API.
flags.evidence: Current surface is defined in src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts:107-125 and returned directly by src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useJoinClipsSettings.ts:9-17, while the plan only says the adapter will 'likely' preserve some legacy fields.

flags.concern: Hook boundary: the proposed factory callbacks cannot call `useToolSettings` directly, so the plan needs an explicit non-hook load/save path and hook-level React Query adapter.
flags.evidence: useToolSettings is a hook in src/shared/hooks/settings/useToolSettings.ts:133-286, but createEntityStore expects plain async `load` and `save` functions in src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts:8-15.

flags.concern: Text-field persistence: current Join Clips callers update prompt fields via `updateField`, so introducing `textFieldKeys` without caller rewiring would change save behavior.
flags.evidence: Prompt setters in src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx:96-103 call `joinSettings.updateField(...)`, and there is no existing `flushTextFields` integration in the Join Clips UI.
